title: Quiz: Kernel Estimation
description: Test your understanding of quantum kernel estimation

questions.question: In the swap test, what is P(ancilla = 0) in terms of the kernel k = |⟨ψ₁|ψ₂⟩|²?
questions.explanation: The swap test gives P(ancilla = 0) = (1 + |⟨ψ₁|ψ₂⟩|²) / 2. To extract the kernel, we compute k = 2P(0) - 1.

questions.question: How many qubits does the compute-uncompute method require compared to the swap test?
questions.explanation: The swap test requires 2n + 1 qubits (two copies of the state plus an ancilla), while compute-uncompute only needs n qubits (single register). This makes compute-uncompute more practical for NISQ devices.

questions.question: How does the number of shots needed scale with target error ε?
questions.explanation: The standard error scales as 1/√n, so to achieve error ε, we need n = O(1/ε²) shots. Halving the error requires quadrupling the shots.

questions.question: Statistical noise in kernel estimation can cause the kernel matrix to become non-positive-semi-definite.
questions.explanation: True. Measurement noise can introduce errors that make eigenvalues slightly negative, violating the PSD property required for valid kernels. This must be corrected before using the matrix in kernel methods.

questions.question: For which kernel value is the estimation variance highest?
questions.explanation: The variance of a Bernoulli random variable is p(1-p), which is maximized when p = 0.5. For kernel estimation, this means k = 0.5 has the highest variance and requires the most shots for accurate estimation.
